summary: A small square, tiered pavilion-like structure with layered horizontal bands of different block materials, a raised central tower/lantern, and an outer low decorative wall of white blocks. The building is largely symmetrical on its main axes and sits centered inside a cleared dirt plot.

shape.symmetry: approximate four-way symmetry (minor asymmetry at one corner entrance)

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: white vertical pillars and outer low wall (uncertain exact white block)

materials.name: glowstone
materials.role: decorative lights on roof corners and center

materials.name: slab_wood
materials.role: possible small trim under white cap

elements: raised central tower/lantern with stacked slabs and glowstone
outer low white decorative border/wall (single-block high with gaps)
stone slab roof edging and flat dark roof surface
rebuild_hints: Start with a square footprint ~15x15 blocks with a low stone foundation band one block high around base.
Stack three main horizontal wall tiers: lower stone/stonebrick trim, middle brick band, upper white capped band with a dark flat roof on top edged by stone slabs and glowstone at corners.
Place four taller white vertical pillars at corners/near entrances rising above the upper band; add shorter white single-block outer border spaced with one-block gaps around base.
Create a centered small tower on the roof: a narrow column rising 3-4 blocks topped by two concentric small flat slab platforms and a glowstone on top.
Use wooden plank band between brick and white bands as a decorative stripe.
uncertainties: Exact block type for the white pillars and outer border: appears to be quartz or white concrete/snow; quartz assumed but not certain.
Precise footprint size and interior layout are not visible; width/depth estimated from aerial views.
Some trim pieces (dark flat roof material and thin horizontal slat) could be netherbrick or dark wood slabs; identification uncertain.
Windows or openings are not clearly visible; presence of glass is uncertain.
